items.example: I am working on a new project.
I have five years of experience.
I work for an international company.
I work in a small team.
I want to improve my English.
My goal is to become a senior engineer.

exercises.label: Basic (Practice 1)
lines: I work for a tech company.
I have three years of experience.

exercises.label: Extended (Practice 2)
lines: I'm a software engineer at a tech company.
Currently, I am working on a cloud project.
I have about five years of experience in this field.
I enjoy solving technical problems and working with my team.
My goal is to improve my skills and become a senior engineer.

sections.title: Sentence Building
sections.subtitle: Complete the sentences using the prompts

items.answer: I have three years of experience.
I want to improve my English speaking skills.
My goal is to communicate in English fluently.
I'm working on a marketing software project.

items.question: How many years of experience do you have?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Hi, I don't think we've met before.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Mark. I'm a software engineer.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Nice to meet you. What do you work on?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: I develop software services for cloud applications.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: How long have you worked here?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: I have about three years of experience.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Sure. My name is Mark. I'm a software engineer from Taiwan.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: What is your goal this year?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: My goal is to improve my English and technical skills.

sections.title: Transition Phrases
sections.subtitle: Use these to make your introduction flow better

items.example: Currently, I am working on a cloud project.
In my free time, I study English.
In the future, I hope to lead a development team.

items.detail: Always use 'a' before job titles: "I am a software engineer" (not "I am software engineer")
"I work for a tech company" or "I work at a tech company" — both are correct
items.chinese: 'work for' 和 'work at' 都可以

items.detail: "My goal is to + verb": My goal is to become a staff engineer.
items.chinese: 目標後面接 to + 動詞

items.detail: "Currently, I am working on..." uses present continuous for ongoing projects.
